original: Brightest cosmic explosion ever seen - a gamma ray burst 2.4 billion light years away, thought to be a massive star collapsing to form a black hole in a 1 in 10,000 year event
A 13 kilogram (est.) meteorite lands in the driveway of Knapp residence, Peekskill, New York, destroying family's 1980 Chevrolet Malibu
World record set for heaviest pumpkin, a jack-o’-lantern gourd weighing 2,749 pounds, grown by Travis Gienger from Anoka, Minnesota  - enough for 687 pies
Astronaut Kathy Sullivan becomes 1st US woman to walk in space during a Space Shuttle Challenger mission (STS-41-G)
Pete Doctor, American filmmaker (Up; Monsters, Inc.) and chief creative officer at Pixar (2018-), born in Bloomington, Minnesota
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to David Baker for building new kinds of proteins, andDemis Hassabisand John Jumper for developing an AI model to predict proteins’ complex structures
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to John B. Goodenough, M. Stanley Whittingham and Akira Yoshino for development of lithium-ion batteries, Goodenough oldest-ever winner at 97
Leif Ericson reaches "Vinland" (possibly L'Anse aux Meadows, Canada), reputedly becoming the first European to reach North America
The last flight of the US aircraft Lockheed SR-71 "Blackbird"
First lunar impact of the Centaur and LCROSS spacecrafts as part of NASA's Lunar Precursor Robotic Program
Beatles' "Yesterday" single goes #1 & stays #1 for 4 weeks
Korean Hangul alphabet is first published by King Sejong the Great
Earliest mention of Prague's astronomical clock, the world's oldest still in operation, built by Mikuláš of Kadaň and Jan Šindel
HooverDam begins transmitting electricity to Los Angeles
Peter Mansfield, British Physician whose discoveries led to MRI (2003 Nobel Prize), born in London (d. 2017)

Part of the lost Piri Reis World Map is rediscovered in the Topkapi Palace Library in Istanbul. Compiled by Ottoman general Piri Reis in 1513, it is the earliest known map of the voyages ofChristopher Columbus
Great meteorite seen from Kentucky to New York
World's largest offshore windfarm, Dogger Bank off the coast of Yorkshire, starts generating power for the UK electricity grid
Joshua Stoddard of Worcester, Massachusetts, patents first calliope (steam powered musical instrument)
American inventorIsaac Singerpatents the sewing machine motor
1st transcontinental flight by a woman completed, Laura Ingalls
NASA civilian test pilot John B McKay takes X-15 to 39,200 m
Joe Rosenthal, American photographer (Pulitzer Prize-Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima), born in Washington, D.C. (d. 2006)
"Strawberry Fields" memorial toJohn Lennonin Central Park, NYC is dedicated by mayorEd Koch
Radio stations around world play "Imagine" honoringJohn Lennonon what would have been his 50th birthday
Soviet dissidentAndrei Sakharovwins Nobel Peace Prize
Serge Haroche and David Wineland win the 2012 Nobel Prize in Physics for work on quantum optics
The UN's World Food Programme is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize
Hurricane Flora ravages Cuba & Haiti, kills 6,000
Hurricane Milton makes landfall near Siesta Key, Florida, as a category 3 storm causing at least 23 deaths amid one of the state's largest-ever evacuations
8.3 magnitude earthquake shakes Cape Yakataga, Alaska
Volcanic eruptions on Tristan de Cunha (South Atlantic)
Baseball World Series: NY Yankees beat St. Louis Cardinals 7-3 at Sportsman's Park to become the first team to sweep consecutive World Series;Babe Ruthsmashes 3 HRs for the Yanks
Gil Anderson races auto (165.1 km record) in Sheepshead Bay, New York
P.J. Harvey, English musician, singer and songwriter, born in Bridport, Dorset, England
Jody Williams, American political activist and aid worker (Nobel Peace Prize 1997), born in Rutland, Vermont
First NFL game coached by an African American (Art Shell), his LA Raiders beat NY Jets 14-7 on Monday Night Football
Cowboy QB Eddie LeBaron throws shortest touchdown pass (2")
Cleveland Rams and Chicago Bears play one of only 4 penalty free games in NFL history; Rams win 14-7 at Cleveland Stadium
Brock Purdymakes his regular-season debut in the NFL
Dennis Eckersley, 1st to save all 4 games in a championship series
American runner Emily Sisson runs US record 2:18:29 in Chicago Marathon, finishing 2nd by 4:11 to repeat winner Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya
German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelfinishes 3rd in the Japanese F1 Grand Prix to clinch his second consecutive World Drivers Championship
Dutch Red Bull driverMax Verstappensecures his second consecutive World F1 Drivers C'ship after finishing in front of Sergio Pérez and Charles Leclerc in the Japanese GP at Suzuka
San Jose Sharks beat Calgary for their 1st NHL win, 4-3
"Hail! Hail! Rock 'n' Roll" documentary film directed by Taylor Hackford of 1986 concerts celebrating rock and roll pioneerChuck Berryis released
Rod Temperton, British pop musician and songwriter ("Boogie Nights"; "Thriller"; "Give Me the Night"; "Yah Mo B There"), born in Cleethorpes, Lincolnshire, England (d. 2016)
Ini Kamoze [Cecil Campbell], Jamaican reggae singer ("Here Comes the Hotstepper"), born in Saint Mary's, Jamaica
John Entwistle, English rock bass guitarist and vocalist (The Who - "Boris The Spider"), born in London, England (d. 2002)
Nona Hendryx, American R&B singer-songwriter (Patti LaBelle & Blue Belles; LaBelle - "Lady Marmalade"), born in Trenton, New Jersey
(Dionisio) "Chucho" Valdés, Cuban jazz and Afro-Cuban pianist, composer, and bandleader (Orquesta Cubana de Música Moderna; Irakere), born in Quivicán, Cuba
Mike Peters, American Pulitzer Prize-winning editorial cartoonist, and comic strip creator (Mother Goose and Grimm), born in St. Louis, Missouri
James Fearnley, British celtic-punk accordionist (The Pogues - "The Irish Rover"), born in Worsley, Greater Manchester, England
Steve McQueen, British filmmaker and artist (12 Years a Slave), born in London, England
Steve Ovett, British athlete (Olympics gold 800m, bronze 1500m 1980), born in Brighton, England
(Isabelle) "France" Gall, French pop singer (Eurovision, 1967 - "Poupee de cire, poupee de son"; "Donner pour donner"; "Sacré Charlemagne"), born in Paris, France (d. 2018)
Pat Burke, British rock saxophonist (Foundations - "Baby Now That I've Found You"; "Build Me Up Buttercup"), born in Kingston, Jamaica, British West Indies
The Supremesrelease single "Someday We'll Be Together",Diana Ross' last recording with the group
11" (28.4 cm) rainfall in 24 hrs (NYC)
First telephone conversation between occupants of a moving car and an airplane
